cards.front: Three ways to configure an IPv6 address on a Cisco interface — show syntax for each.
cards.back: Manual full: `ipv6 address 2001:db8:1::1/64`. EUI-64 derived: `ipv6 address 2001:db8:1::/64 eui-64`. Link-local only: `ipv6 enable` (auto-creates fe80::/64 link-local; no global address). Multiple GUAs are allowed per interface — each with its own subnet.

cards.front: How do you set a specific link-local address on an interface?
cards.back: `ipv6 address fe80::1 link-local`. Useful for predictable next-hop in routing tables (e.g., OSPFv3). Otherwise, link-local is auto-derived from EUI-64 and harder to remember.

cards.front: How do you verify IPv6 interfaces and addresses on Cisco?
cards.back: `show ipv6 interface brief` (compact: name, status, addresses). `show ipv6 interface gi0/0` (detailed: link-local, joined multicast groups, MTU, NDP info). `show ipv6 route` (routing table).

cards.front: What multicast group does an IPv6 interface JOIN automatically when configured?
cards.back: (1) ff02::1 (all nodes — every IPv6 host). (2) ff02::1:ffXX:XXXX (solicited-node, derived from last 24 bits of each unicast address). Routers also join ff02::2 (all routers). OSPFv3 joins ff02::5 / ff02::6.

cards.front: Configure an IPv6 static route to 2001:db8:2::/64 via next-hop 2001:db8:0::2.
cards.back: `ipv6 route 2001:db8:2::/64 2001:db8:0::2`. With exit interface (recommended for link-local next-hops): `ipv6 route 2001:db8:2::/64 gi0/1 fe80::1`. Default route: `ipv6 route ::/0 <next-hop>`.

cards.front: What's the trick about IPv6 static routes with link-local next-hops?
cards.back: You MUST specify the exit interface, because link-local addresses are only meaningful within a single link (fe80::1 could exist on dozens of interfaces). Format: `ipv6 route <prefix> <interface> <link-local-next-hop>`.
